title: evea axillaris
intro: Evea axillaris, also known as the Indian spurge or Chaulmoogra tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and various health benefits. The plant contains a compound called hypophyllanthin, which has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as leprosy and other skin infections. Evea axillaris has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Evea axillaris are typically made into a paste or oil, which can be applied topically to affected areas, or consumed as a tea to support overall health and well-being.
